explanation: The property states that "Globally, if 'DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component' happens, then 'this component must send the display message to the User Interface' must eventually happen." This is captured by a global implication (G(A -> F B)) where the antecedent is the trigger event (bool_exp1) and the consequent is an eventual fulfillment (F bool_exp4) achieved by using the 'until' operator with TRUE as its first argument (TRUE U bool_exp4 is equivalent to F bool_exp4). The 'for every DMi' indicates the global ('G') operator.

The property describes a global response pattern: whenever 'DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component' (bool_exp1) occurs, it must eventually be followed by 'the Services component sending the display message to the User Interface' (bool_exp3). Template 48, G !bool_exp1 | F (bool_exp1 & F[0,] bool_exp3), precisely captures this 'Globally, if P then eventually Q' (G(P -> F Q)) pattern, where the implication is expressed as 'always not P, or eventually P occurs and then eventually Q occurs'.

The natural language statement describes a response property: whenever 'DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component' (bool_exp1), it must be followed by 'this component sends the display message to the User Interface' (bool_exp3). The phrase 'This property holds for every DMi' indicates that this is a global requirement. Template 48, G !bool_exp1 | F (bool_exp1 & F[0,] bool_exp3), precisely captures this pattern, which is logically equivalent to G (bool_exp1 -> F bool_exp3).

The property describes a global response pattern: whenever 'DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component' (trigger), 'this component must send the display message to the User Interface' (response) must eventually occur. Template 105, 'G (bool_exp3 -> (!FALSE) U[0,] (bool_exp4))', accurately captures this. Here, bool_exp3 represents the trigger, and bool_exp4 represents the required response. The (!FALSE) part of the 'Until' operator simplifies to 'TRUE', making the expression equivalent to 'G (bool_exp3 -> F bool_exp4)'. The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures the property holds at all times, satisfying 'This property holds for every DMi'.

The property states that whenever 'DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component' (bool_exp1) occurs, it must always be the case that 'this component must send the display message to the User Interface' (bool_exp3) holds from that point onwards. This is captured by a global implication (G (A -> G B)), where A is the trigger and G B means B must always hold after A. Template 28 directly matches this pattern.

The property states that whenever the 'dui_sends_local_matrix_to_services' event occurs (expressed as bool_exp3), it must eventually be followed by 'services_component_sends_display_msg_to_ui' (expressed as bool_exp4). The 'G' operator captures the 'This property holds for every DMi' aspect, ensuring this behavior is globally true. The '(!FALSE) U[0,] (bool_exp4)' part signifies that bool_exp4 must eventually become true at some point in the future after bool_exp3 is true.

The property states that whenever 'DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component' occurs, it must eventually be followed by 'this component must send the display message to the User Interface'. The phrase 'This property holds for every DMi' indicates a global (G) constraint. Template 48, 'G !bool_exp1 | F (bool_exp1 & F[0,] bool_exp3)', captures this 'globally, if an event occurs, then another event must eventually occur' pattern. This template is equivalent to 'G (bool_exp1 -> F bool_exp3)'.

The property states that globally, whenever 'DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component', it must eventually be followed by 'the Services component sending the display message to the User Interface'. This directly captures the 'Once A, then B must happen' requirement, and the 'This property holds for every DMi' phrase implies the global (G) operator.

The property states that globally, whenever 'DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component' (bool_exp3) occurs, it must eventually be followed by 'this component must send the display message to the User Interface' (bool_exp4). The template 105, G (bool_exp3 -> (!FALSE) U[0,] (bool_exp4)), captures this by ensuring that if bool_exp3 holds, bool_exp4 must eventually hold, as (!FALSE) U[0,] bool_exp4 simplifies to F bool_exp4. The 'G' operator addresses the 'This property holds for every DMi' aspect by making it a global requirement, implying it applies universally to any instance of the described event for any DMi.

The property describes a cause-effect relationship: "Once DUi sends the local matrix to the Services component, this component must send the display message to the User Interface". The phrase "This property holds for every DMi" implies a global (G) temporal operator. The "once... then must" structure indicates that if the first event (A) occurs, then the second event (B) must eventually (F) happen. This translates to the LTL pattern G (A -> F B). Template 48, G !bool_exp1 | F (bool_exp1 & F[0,] bool_exp3), is logically equivalent to G (bool_exp1 -> F[0,] bool_exp3). Here, bool_exp1 represents the triggering event 'dui_sends_local_matrix_to_services', and bool_exp3 represents the consequent event 'services_component_sends_display_msg_to_ui'. The F[0,] operator means 'eventually' without a specific upper time bound.
